text: The surgeon provides a view of the contracted tumor located in the middle of the sigmoid colon, avoiding direct contact with the tumor. The sigmoid colon below the tumor extends to the rectosigmoid junction, surrounded by abundant visceral fat. The assistant uses the fourth robotic arm to pull the tissue upward towards the patient's head, aiding in retraction.
The surgeon begins the medial to lateral dissection of the sigmoid colon, navigating through the abundant mesenteric fat while carefully retracting surrounding tissues.
The surgeon connects the lateral and medial dissections, ensuring the ureter is identified and protected. The use of stents or ICG in the ureters is mentioned as a selective practice in these cases.
The surgeon maneuvers around the surgical field without yet addressing the vessels, indicating the proximal resection margin while navigating the challenging medial to lateral approach.

The surgeon flips the colon medially and navigates through a large amount of mesenteric fat to access the proximal colon. The inferior mesenteric artery is ligated, with careful attention to avoid losing track of the vessels and causing bleeding. The surgeon progresses towards the proximal resection margin.

The surgeon, having taken the vessels and reached the proximal colon, returns to the rectosigmoid junction to select the distal resection margin. The dissection is taken lower than necessary for transection to aid in anastomosis, ensuring mobility and alignment of the rectum. The mesentery is meticulously cleaned off to prevent it from being incorporated into the staple line. Indocyanine green (ICG) is used for visualization.

The surgeon confirms the proximal and distal perfusion and proceeds to make a Pfannenstiel incision to extract the specimen, which remains attached proximally. A purse string suture is placed, and the anvil is positioned. Due to the large incision required for the specimen, the assistant inserts a hand through a gel port to assist with the anastomosis.
The surgeon completes the anastomosis and performs a leak test using a rigid proctoscope, ensuring the anastomosis is open and not too tight.
